lede: A look at December 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Michael Olise and Pat Calathes, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Grandmaster (chess)
events.desc: Indian Grandmaster Gukesh Dommaraju became the undisputed World Chess Champion in a tournament held in Singapore, making him the 18th and the youngest champion in chess history.

events.title: Supreme Court of the United States
events.desc: The United States Supreme Court releases its decision in Bush v. Gore.

events.title: Clapham Junction rail crash
events.desc: The Clapham Junction rail crash kills thirty-five and injures hundreds after two collisions of three commuter trains—one of the worst train crashes in the United Kingdom.

events.title: 1979 Tumaco earthquake
events.desc: The 8.2 Mw Tumaco earthquake shakes Colombia and Ecuador with a maximum Mercalli intensity of IX (Violent), killing 300–600, and generating a large tsunami.

events.title: Piazza Fontana bombing
events.desc: The Piazza Fontana bombing; a bomb explodes at the headquarters of Banca Nazionale dell'Agricoltura (the National Agricultural Bank) in Piazza Fontana in Milan, Italy, killing 17 people and wounding 88. The same afternoon, three more bombs are detonated in Rome and Milan, and another is found unexploded.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Fifty-four Japanese A6M Zero fighters raid Batangas Field, Philippines. Jesús Villamor and four Filipino fighter pilots fend them off; César Basa is killed.

events.title: The Holocaust
events.desc: The Holocaust: Adolf Hitler declares the imminent extermination of the Jews at a meeting in the Reich Chancellery.

events.title: Xi'an Incident
events.desc: The beginning of Xi'an incident. As a result, Chiang Kai Shek is captured.

events.title: Oaks explosion
events.desc: Oaks explosion: The worst mining disaster in England kills 361 miners and rescuers.

events.title: Battle of Nineveh (627)
events.desc: Battle of Nineveh: A Byzantine army under Emperor Heraclius defeats Emperor Khosrau II's Persian forces, commanded by General Rhahzadh.

deaths.text: Vicente Fernández, Mexican actor, ranchera singer, and film producer
Norman Bridwell, American author and illustrator, created Clifford the Big Red Dog
Ike Turner, American singer-songwriter, guitarist, and producer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Jane Frances de Chantal
